title: Animating Git visualizations with gifski
text: recipes also assume data-collection ggplot-based plot helpers ghreadme:
code: install.packages(c("gifski", "ggplot2")) library(ghreadme)  commits <- collect_git_commits(   user   = "your-username",   emails = c("you@example.com", "you@work.example.com") )

title: Animating Git visualizations with gifski
text: recipe follows pattern: Create temporary directory PNG frames. Loop, building one ggplot per frame writing ggsave(). Call make_gif() assemble frames GIF. make_gif() exported ghreadme handles collecting PNGs, building per-frame delay vector, calling gifski::gifski(). first_duration last_duration arguments let hold opening closing frames longer readers time register start end states. three recipes follow pattern differ plotted frame.

what: Recipe 1: cumulative commits, sliding the end date
title: Animating Git visualizations with gifski
text: cumulative_line_plot() already shows growth time, stepping date_end forward one month time makes growth feel kinetic — lines appear, lengthen, overtake one another repos accumulate commits. Note: Start date_seq one month date_begin. date_end equals date_begin first iteration commits exist exact day, cumulative_line_plot() error “commits filtering.”
code: date_seq <- seq(   from = as.Date("2023-02-01"),  # one month after date_begin   to   = max(commits$date),   by   = "1 month" )  cum_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "cumulative-frames") dir.create(cum_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  for (i in seq_along(date_seq)) {   p <- cumulative_line_plot(     commits,     date_begin = "2023-01-01",     date_end   = date_seq[[i]],     top_n      = 8   )   ggsave(     filename = file.path(cum_dir, sprintf("%04d.png", i)),     plot     = p,     width = 1000, height = 600, units = "px", dpi = 96, bg = "white"   ) }  make_gif(cum_dir, "cumulative.gif",          width = 1000, height = 600,          first_duration = 1, frame_duration = 0.4, last_duration = 4)

what: Recipe 2: calendar heatmap, year by year
title: Animating Git visualizations with gifski
text: calendar_heatmap_plot() strong fit slow reveal: frame adds one year history, GIF builds toward present. plot’s date_begin argument honoured filtering, need bump date_end end next year.
code: years <- sort(unique(commits$year))  cal_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "calendar-frames") dir.create(cal_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  for (i in seq_along(years)) {   p <- calendar_heatmap_plot(     commits,     date_begin = paste0(min(years), "-01-01"),     date_end   = paste0(years[[i]], "-12-31"),     title      = paste("Through", years[[i]])   )   ggsave(     filename = file.path(cal_dir, sprintf("%04d.png", i)),     plot     = p,     width = 1100, height = 700, units = "px", dpi = 96, bg = "white"   ) }  make_gif(cal_dir, "calendar.gif",          width = 1100, height = 700,          first_duration = 1, frame_duration = 1.2, last_duration = 4)

title: Animating Git visualizations with gifski
text: third pattern cycles repositories instead time — punchcard_plot() rendered per repo, GIF feels like flipping deck cards.
code: top_repos <- commits |>   dplyr::count(repo, sort = TRUE) |>   dplyr::slice_head(n = 6) |>   dplyr::pull(repo)  punch_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "punchcard-frames") dir.create(punch_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  for (i in seq_along(top_repos)) {   p <- punchcard_plot(commits, repo = top_repos[[i]], title = top_repos[[i]])   ggsave(     filename = file.path(punch_dir, sprintf("%04d.png", i)),     plot     = p,     width = 1000, height = 500, units = "px", dpi = 96, bg = "white"   ) }  make_gif(punch_dir, "punchcard.gif",          width = 1000, height = 500,          first_duration = 1, frame_duration = 1.5, last_duration = 4)

title: Animating Git visualizations with gifski
text: Frame count vs file size. GIFs uncompressed across frames, monthly cadence five years (60 frames) renders nicely; daily cadence produces multi-megabyte file. Pick step yields 20–80 frames. Final-frame hold. last_duration (seconds) gives readers time absorb end state GIF loops. 3–5 seconds good range. Consistent axes. plots auto-scale per frame, makes axes jitter data added. bothers , pass explicit date_begin /date_end x-axis stays fixed across frames. Spiral plots. spiral_points_plot(), spiral_heatmap_plot(), spiral_horizon_plot() draw grid/spiralize graphics system rather returning ggplot objects. already compatible ggsave() + gifski::gifski() approach used — save frame grDevices::png() / dev.() pass resulting file list directly gifski::gifski().

title: Animating Git visualizations with gifski

code: @Manual{,   title = {ghreadme: Create a GitHub profile README},   author = {Martin Frigaard},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://mjfrigaard.github.io/ghreadme/}, }

title: Create a GitHub profile README
text: goal ghreadme create GitHub profile README.md using R Markdown.

title: Create a GitHub profile README
text: can install development version ghreadme like :
code: remotes::install_github("mjfrigaard/ghreadme")

title: Create a GitHub profile README
text: Load package, create repo name username (.e., https://github.com/<username>/<username>), add following README.Rmd file (set echo=FALSE hide code).
code: library(ghreadme) who_am_i(name = "Martin",   likes = "#rstats and data visualization.",   learn = "Shiny app development, Python, and Linux",   work = "R package development tools.",   collab = "#rstats packages for data science."   ) 👋 Hi, my name is Martin.       👀 I like #rstats and data visualization.       🌱 I'm learning about Shiny app development, Python, and Linux       📦 I'm currently working on R package development tools.       💞 I'd love to collaborate on #rstats packages for data science.       📫 Want to connect? Use the badges below...

title: Create a GitHub profile README
text: GitHub badges vercel.app, include call code chunk results set asis. Example:
code: ```{r gh_badges, results='asis'} gh_badges(username = "mjfrigaard",    badge = c("details", "stats", "repo_lang"),    theme = "dark") ```

title: Create a GitHub profile README
text: Example:
code: ```{r so_rep, results='asis'} so_rep(   username = "martin-frigaard",    user_id = "4926446") ```

what: Assemble a directory of PNG frames into a GIF — make_gif
title: Assemble a directory of PNG frames into a GIF — make_gif
text: Collects every .png file frame_dir (sorted alphabetically), builds per-frame delay vector distinct hold times first last frames, writes result GIF via gifski::gifski().

title: Assemble a directory of PNG frames into a GIF — make_gif
code: make_gif(   frame_dir,   output,   width,   height,   first_duration = 1,   frame_duration = 0.4,   last_duration = 4 )

title: Assemble a directory of PNG frames into a GIF — make_gif
text: frame_dir Path directory containing numbered .png frame files. output Path output .gif file (e.g. "animation.gif"). width GIF width pixels. height GIF height pixels. first_duration Seconds hold first frame (default 1). frame_duration Seconds show middle frame (default 0.4). last_duration Seconds hold last frame (default 4).

title: Assemble a directory of PNG frames into a GIF — make_gif
text: Invisibly returns output.

title: Assemble a directory of PNG frames into a GIF — make_gif
text: typical workflow : Create temporary directory. Loop data, saving one plot per frame ggplot2::ggsave(). Call make_gif() assemble frames.

title: Assemble a directory of PNG frames into a GIF — make_gif
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ frame_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "my-frames") dir.create(frame_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)  for (i in 1:5) {   p <- ggplot2::ggplot() + ggplot2::ggtitle(paste("Frame", i))   ggplot2::ggsave(     filename = file.path(frame_dir, sprintf("%04d.png", i)),     plot     = p,     width    = 800,     height   = 400,     units    = "px"   ) }  make_gif(frame_dir, "animation.gif", width = 800, height = 400) } # }

NULL uses repos. date_begin, date_end Optional Date bounds. x, y One "repo", "time", "date", "weekday" (must differ). plot_type "ggplot", "plotly", "density" (ggplot + marginal densities). top_n repo axis repo arg NULL, keep many -active repos (rest dropped keep axis readable). title Plot title.

what: Create a GIF of who_am_i() console output — who_am_i_gif
title: Create a GIF of who_am_i() console output — who_am_i_gif
text: Records message who_am_i() ggplot frame using ggplot2 ggsave(), assembles frames GIF gifski.

title: Create a GIF of who_am_i() console output — who_am_i_gif
code: who_am_i_gif(   name,   likes,   learn,   work,   collab,   connect = TRUE,   output = "intro.gif",   width = 800,   height = 400,   frame_duration = 1.5,   first_duration = 1,   last_duration = 4 )

title: Create a GIF of who_am_i() console output — who_am_i_gif
text: name name likes likes learn learning work currently working collab like collaborate connect logical, include connect message (default TRUE) output path output gif (default "intro.gif") width gif width pixels (default 800) height gif height pixels (default 400) frame_duration seconds middle frame shown (default 1.5) first_duration seconds first frame shown (default 1) last_duration seconds last frame shown (default 4)

title: Create a GIF of who_am_i() console output — who_am_i_gif
